wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the AI-Based Clinical Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS). Explain its purpose as a healthcare-focused cybersecurity solution, architecture overview, and relationship to clinical network environments. Document the system's core functionality including real-time network traffic monitoring, AI-powered intrusion detection using XGBoost, SHAP explainability, and interactive dashboard analytics. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including NIDS, SHAP, XGBoost, and clinical network security. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases in healthcare environments. Document the microservices architecture connecting backend API, frontend dashboard, and ML service components.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application architecture. Document the routing system using React Router, component hierarchy with Layout wrapper, sidebar navigation structure, and top navigation bar implementation. Explain the overall application flow, component composition patterns, and how different pages are organized under the main Layout component. Include details about the routing configuration, protected routes, and navigation patterns. Document the component lifecycle, prop drilling strategies, and state sharing between components.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI-Based Clinical Network Intrusion Detection System introduction. Explain the system's purpose as a healthcare-focused cybersecurity solution designed to protect clinical networks from cyber threats. Document the core problem it solves in healthcare environments, including the critical need for real-time network monitoring and AI-powered threat detection. Describe the system's mission to provide accurate intrusion detection with explainable AI for medical device networks. Include the target audience from healthcare IT administrators to cybersecurity analysts. Use terminology consistent with clinical network security and healthcare regulations. Provide context on why traditional NIDS solutions are insufficient for healthcare environments and how this system addresses those gaps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication API documentation covering JWT-based user authentication system. Document the /api/auth/login endpoint including request/response schemas, validation rules, and authentication flow. Explain JWT token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Detail the security filter implementation, CORS configuration, and password encoding with BCrypt. Include authentication middleware integration, role-based access control, and session management. Provide practical examples of login requests, token handling, and secure API consumption patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: ML Engine Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ML engine architecture. Explain the prediction engine design patterns, factory pattern implementation for predictor creation, and integration with XGBoost models. Document the NIDSPredictor class structure, feature preprocessing pipeline, and model loading mechanisms. Detail the prediction workflow from raw features to final classification, including confidence scoring and severity assessment. Explain the SHAP explanation generation process, feature importance calculation, and explainability features. Include code examples showing engine initialization, prediction execution, and result interpretation. Address performance optimization techniques and memory management strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core domain entities including User, Detection, and Alert models. Document field definitions, data types, validation constraints, and business logic for each entity. Explain the User entity's role-based access control system, Detection entity's intrusion detection results with confidence scores, and Alert entity's security alert management. Include entity lifecycle methods, creation timestamps, and relationship mappings. Provide examples of entity instantiation, common operations, and integration patterns with the security system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation covering prerequisites, environment setup, and initial configuration. Document system requirements including Java 17+, Node.js, Python 3.x, and database setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for all three components: Spring Boot backend, React frontend, and FastAPI ML service. Include database configuration for PostgreSQL/H2, environment variable setup, and service startup procedures using the provided batch script. Document common installation issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide verification steps to ensure all components are running correctly and can communicate with each other.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the core dashboard pages including Dashboard analytics, Analytics overview, and Real-time Monitoring interfaces. Document the data visualization components, chart implementations using Recharts, real-time data fetching patterns, and interactive dashboard widgets. Explain the analytics data structures, metric calculations, and KPI displays. Cover the monitoring page's live traffic visualization, threat detection indicators, and system status displays. Include examples of chart configurations, data binding patterns, and real-time update mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the AI-Based Clinical NIDS system. Describe the high-level microservices architecture consisting of three main components: Spring Boot backend API service, React frontend dashboard, and FastAPI machine learning service. Document component interactions, data flows between services, and integration patterns. Explain the technology stack choices including Java Spring Boot for backend, React for frontend, and Python FastAPI for ML processing. Include system context diagrams showing how network traffic flows through the ML service for prediction, how results are stored in the database, and how the dashboard presents analytics. Address the startup orchestration approach using the batch script and service dependencies. Document scalability considerations and deployment topology options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Detection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed detection management API documentation covering real-time network traffic analysis and intrusion detection. Document endpoints for retrieving detection results, managing alerts, and accessing attack details. Explain the integration with the ML service for prediction processing, data persistence patterns, and real-time monitoring capabilities. Include request/response schemas for detection queries, alert filtering, and statistical analytics. Detail the detection lifecycle, confidence scoring, and automated alert generation based on threat severity levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the data processing pipeline. Explain feature engineering processes, data validation procedures, and preprocessing workflows. Document the TrafficFeatures model mapping, feature name transformations, and data type conversions. Detail the dataset validation logic, missing value handling, and outlier detection mechanisms. Explain the Parquet file processing, batch prediction workflows, and result formatting. Include examples of data transformation pipelines, feature scaling techniques, and quality assurance checks. Address data integrity validation, error handling strategies, and performance optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Analysis Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for dataset analysis related entities including DatasetAnalysis, NetworkTraffic, and PredictionResult models. Document the dataset upload workflow, batch processing capabilities, and prediction result storage. Explain NetworkTraffic entity's data capture from PCAP files, DatasetAnalysis entity's statistical analysis results, and PredictionResult entity's individual flow predictions with SHAP explanations. Include data validation rules, batch processing workflows, and integration with the machine learning service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the alert management system including the Alerts listing page and Threat Details view. Document the alert data structure, severity classification system, filtering and sorting capabilities, and alert resolution workflows. Explain the threat details page implementation with attack pattern visualization, timeline analysis, and remediation recommendations. Include examples of alert categorization, bulk operations, notification systems, and integration with the backend alert API endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core features of the AI-Based Clinical NIDS system. Thoroughly explain the real-time network traffic monitoring capabilities, AI-powered intrusion detection using XGBoost algorithms, SHAP-based explainable AI for detection decisions, and interactive dashboard analytics. Document the dataset upload and batch analysis functionality, user authentication and authorization system, and PDF report generation capabilities. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how these features work together to provide comprehensive network security monitoring. Explain the relationship between features and their role in healthcare cybersecurity workflows. Address common use cases and scenarios where each feature provides value in clinical network environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive dataset operation API documentation covering batch analysis workflows and data processing pipelines. Document endpoints for dataset upload (/api/upload), batch prediction processing, and analysis result retrieval (/api/analyze/{id}). Explain the dataset validation process, preprocessing requirements, and integration with the ML service for batch inference. Include file upload handling, progress tracking, result formatting, and report generation capabilities. Detail the analysis workflow from upload through completion, error handling for invalid datasets, and result export options.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all ML service endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Detail the prediction endpoints (/api/predict, /api/predict/batch), dataset operations (/api/upload, /api/analyze/{id}), and utility endpoints (/api/model/info, /api/health). Include request payload examples, response schemas, error codes, and status messages. Document the traffic simulator endpoints and their usage patterns. Provide curl examples, SDK integration guidelines, and rate limiting information. Address CORS configuration, input validation rules, and security considerations for each endpoint.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attack Detail Entity
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AttackDetail entity that provides detailed information about detected attack types and characteristics. Document the attack classification system, severity scoring, protocol-specific details, and threat categorization. Explain how AttackDetail integrates with Detection entities to provide contextual information about security threats. Include attack type enumerations, protocol mappings, and correlation with network traffic patterns for healthcare environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Spring Boot backend RESTful services. Document all HTTP endpoints including authentication (/api/auth/**), detection management (/api/detections/**), dataset operations (/api/upload/**), alert management (/api/alerts/**), and traffic monitoring (/api/traffic/**). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and example requests/responses. Document the JWT-based authentication system, role-based access control, and security configurations. Provide integration examples and common use cases for each API group.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React-based frontend dashboard application. Document the component architecture, routing system, state management, and integration with backend APIs. Cover all major pages including Dashboard analytics, Real-time Monitoring, Alert management, Dataset Upload interface, and Threat Details view. Document component hierarchies, prop requirements, event handling, and data flow patterns. Include styling implementation with Tailwind CSS, responsive design considerations, and chart integration using Recharts. Provide examples of component usage, customization options, and extension points for adding new dashboard features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the dataset upload and analysis workflow including the DatasetUpload interface and AnalysisResult display pages. Explain the file upload process, validation mechanisms, progress tracking, and batch analysis initiation. Cover the analysis result presentation including statistical summaries, detection metrics, and downloadable reports. Document the user interface for dataset management, upload progress indicators, error handling during uploads, and result interpretation guidelines. Include examples of supported file formats, size limitations, and processing time expectations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the AI-Based Clinical NIDS system. Document the backend technologies including Spring Boot 3.3.1, Java 17, JWT authentication, PostgreSQL/H2 database, and WebFlux reactive programming. Detail the frontend technologies comprising React 18.3.1, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and Recharts for data visualization. Explain the machine learning service stack using FastAPI 0.109.0, XGBoost for ensemble learning, SHAP for explainability, scikit-learn, and pandas for data processing. Include dependency versions, licensing considerations, and technology selection rationale. Document development tools, build systems, and deployment technologies. Address cross-platform compatibility and system requirements for Windows development environment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed alert management API documentation covering security alert generation, filtering, and resolution tracking. Document endpoints for retrieving active alerts, viewing alert history, and managing alert states. Explain the alert severity classification system, automated alert creation from detection results, and manual alert management capabilities. Include filtering by severity, time range, attack type, and resolution status. Detail alert notification mechanisms, escalation workflows, and integration with external monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Training and Evaluation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for model training procedures and evaluation processes. Explain the training data preparation, feature selection strategies, and hyperparameter tuning methods. Document the XGBoost model configuration, training workflows, and cross-validation techniques. Detail performance evaluation metrics, confusion matrices, and ROC curve analysis. Explain the model artifact management, versioning strategies, and deployment procedures. Include examples of training scripts, evaluation workflows, and model comparison techniques. Address overfitting prevention, feature importance analysis, and model interpretability methods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation covering table relationships, foreign key constraints, and database design patterns. Document the relational schema for all entities including primary keys, unique constraints, and indexing strategies. Explain the database configuration for both development (H2) and production (PostgreSQL) environments. Include entity relationship diagrams, common query patterns for healthcare data analysis, and performance optimization techniques. Document data migration strategies, backup procedures, and security considerations for medical data protection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Machine Learning Service Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI-based machine learning service. Document the prediction engine architecture, XGBoost model integration, SHAP explanation generation, and data preprocessing pipeline. Explain the model loading mechanism, feature engineering processes, batch prediction workflows, and real-time inference capabilities. Document the API endpoints for single flow prediction and batch processing, input validation, and result formatting. Include model training procedures, performance evaluation metrics, and artifact management. Provide examples of model usage, integration patterns, and extension possibilities for additional ML algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication interface including the Login page implementation, form validation, and user session management. Document the login form components, input validation rules, error handling, and authentication flow integration with the backend API. Explain the user experience patterns, security considerations, and session persistence mechanisms. Include examples of form submission handling, error state management, and redirect logic after successful authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the AI-Based Clinical NIDS system. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including prerequisites (Java 17+, Node.js, Python), environment setup, and dependency installation. Document the development environment configuration for all three services (backend, frontend, ML service) including database setup and service dependencies. Explain the startup procedure using the provided batch script and manual startup alternatives. Include basic usage examples for accessing the dashboard, uploading datasets, and running initial analyses. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide verification steps to ensure all components are working correctly. Include minimum system requirements and recommended hardware specifications for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Traffic Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive traffic monitoring API documentation covering real-time network traffic capture and analysis. Document endpoints for retrieving live traffic data, historical traffic patterns, and traffic statistics. Explain the traffic data collection process, real-time streaming capabilities, and integration with network monitoring systems. Include filtering options for traffic sources, destinations, protocols, and time ranges. Detail traffic aggregation methods, anomaly detection triggers, and correlation with detection results for comprehensive threat analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Hibernate/JPA entity classes and database schema. Detail all entity relationships including User, Detection, Alert, DatasetAnalysis, NetworkTraffic, and PredictionResult models. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign key relationships, and database constraints. Explain the domain model design, business rules, and data validation requirements. Include entity relationship diagrams, sample data examples, and common query patterns. Document database migration strategies, indexing considerations, and performance optimization techniques for healthcare data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the styling architecture including Tailwind CSS configuration, custom CSS implementation, and theme management. Explain the design system, color schemes, typography choices, and responsive design patterns used throughout the application. Cover the PostCSS configuration, CSS-in-JS patterns, and component styling approaches. Include guidelines for maintaining design consistency, extending styles for new components, and implementing dark/light theme switching if applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security documentation covering the JWT-based authentication system, authorization mechanisms, and security configurations. Document the JWT token generation and validation process, password encoding with BCrypt, CORS configuration, and CSRF protection. Explain the user authentication flow, role-based access control, and session management. Include security best practices, input validation strategies, SQL injection prevention, and secure communication protocols. Document the custom user details service implementation, security filter chain, and integration with the frontend authentication system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation covering environment configuration, containerization, and production setup. Document database connection management, API endpoint configuration, security parameter tuning, and logging configuration. Include Docker containerization strategies, Kubernetes deployment patterns, load balancing considerations, and monitoring setup. Explain development vs production environment differences, configuration management, and secrets handling. Document scaling strategies, backup procedures, and disaster recovery planning for healthcare network security systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation covering unit testing, integration testing, and API endpoint testing strategies. Document testing frameworks used, test organization patterns, and mocking strategies for external dependencies. Include frontend testing methodologies, component testing approaches, and user interaction testing. Document performance testing procedures, security testing considerations, and cross-browser compatibility testing. Provide guidelines for test data management, continuous integration setup, and automated testing pipelines for the healthcare cybersecurity application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and their solutions. Document database connectivity problems, API integration failures, ML model loading errors, and frontend rendering issues. Include debugging techniques, logging configuration, error tracking implementation, and performance profiling methods. Provide diagnostic procedures for system integration issues, authentication problems, and service communication failures. Document frequently asked questions about system configuration, performance optimization, and maintenance procedures specific to healthcare network security environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Clinical-NIDS: AI-Based Clinical Network Intrusion Detection System

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Clinical-NIDS is an advanced AI-based network intrusion detection system specifically designed for healthcare environments. The system provides real-time monitoring, threat detection, and comprehensive analytics for clinical network infrastructure, combining machine learning capabilities with intuitive visualization tools.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Threat Detection**: Monitor and analyze network traffic for suspicious activities in healthcare settings
- **Explainable AI**: Provide SHAP-based explanations for detection decisions to aid cybersecurity analysts
- **Comprehensive Analytics**: Offer detailed insights through interactive dashboards and reports
- **Scalable Architecture**: Support both batch analysis and real-time traffic monitoring
- **Healthcare-Focused Security**: Address unique challenges of clinical network environments

### Target Audience
- Healthcare IT administrators
- Cybersecurity professionals in medical institutions
- Network engineers managing clinical systems
- Security analysts requiring threat intelligence

## Technical Architecture

The Clinical-NIDS system follows a microservices architecture with three main components working in harmony:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        A[React Dashboard] --> B[Navigation]
        A --> C[Components]
    end
    
    subgraph "API Gateway"
        D[Spring Boot Backend] --> E[REST Controllers]
        D --> F[Security Layer]
    end
    
    subgraph "Machine Learning Service"
        G[FastAPI ML Engine] --> H[Prediction Engine]
        G --> I[Feature Processing]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        J[H2 Database] --> K[PostgreSQL]
        L[File Storage] --> M[Uploads]
    end
    
    A --> D
    D --> G
    D --> J
    G --> L
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Dashboard**
- React-based interface with Tailwind CSS styling
- Real-time monitoring capabilities
- Interactive charts and analytics
- Responsive design for various devices

**Backend API Service**
- Spring Boot REST API with comprehensive CRUD operations
- JWT-based authentication and authorization
- Database abstraction layer for persistence
- Integration with machine learning service

**Machine Learning Engine**
- FastAPI-based prediction service
- XGBoost model for intrusion detection
- SHAP-based explainability
- Batch and real-time prediction capabilities

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers
- **Microservices Pattern**: Independent services for ML processing, API management, and frontend
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access through repositories
- **DTO Pattern**: Data transfer objects for API communication
- **Factory Pattern**: Prediction engine abstraction for different data sources

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Frontend Client
    participant API as Spring Boot API
    participant ML as ML Service
    participant DB as Database
    
    Client->>+API: Request Detection
    API->>+ML: Predict Flow
    ML->>ML: Process Features
    ML-->>-API: Prediction Result
    API->>+DB: Save Detection
    DB-->>-API: Confirmation
    API-->>-Client: Response with ID
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application Entry Point**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/ClinicalNidsApplication.java` - Primary Spring Boot application starter

**ML Service Entry Point**
- `Mini_Project/ml-service/app.py` - FastAPI application with comprehensive endpoint definitions

**Frontend Application Entry Point**
- `Mini_Project/clinical-nids-dashboard/src/App.jsx` - React Router configuration and route definitions

### Core Modules

**Security Configuration**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/config/SecurityConfig.java` - JWT authentication and CORS configuration

**Detection Service**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/service/DetectionService.java` - Integration layer between API and ML service

**Prediction Engine**
- `Mini_Project/ml-service/prediction_engine.py` - Core ML processing logic with SHAP explanations

**Data Models**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/entity/Detection.java` - ORM entity for detection records

### Configuration Approach

**Application Properties**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/resources/application.properties` - Multi-environment configuration (development/production)

**Package Dependencies**
- `Mini_Project/backend/pom.xml` - Maven dependency management for Java backend
- `Mini_Project/clinical-nids-dashboard/package.json` - React frontend dependencies
- `Mini_Project/ml-service/requirements.txt` - Python ML service dependencies

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Spring Boot 3.3.1] --> B[Web Starter]
    A --> C[Security Starter]
    A --> D[JPA Starter]
    A --> E[Validation]
    
    F[JWT Library] --> G[JJWT 0.12.5]
    H[Database] --> I[H2 for Dev]
    H --> J[PostgreSQL for Prod]
    K[Lombok] --> L[Code Generation]
    M[OpenPDF] --> N[Report Generation]
```

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 18.3.1 with React DOM for rendering
- React Router DOM for navigation
- Recharts for data visualization
- Tailwind CSS for styling

**ML Service Dependencies**
- FastAPI 0.109.0 for API framework
- Scikit-learn for machine learning algorithms
- XGBoost for ensemble learning
- SHAP for explainable AI
- Pandas and NumPy for data processing

### Integration Points

**Cross-Service Communication**
- RESTful API endpoints for inter-service communication
- JSON-based data exchange format
- Asynchronous processing for batch operations
- WebSocket support for real-time updates

**Startup Orchestration**
- `Mini_Project/start_all.bat` - Automated service startup script
- Sequential service initialization
- Port-based service coordination

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class DetectionController {
        +predict(TrafficRequest) Map
        +getDetections(int) List
        +getStatistics() DashboardStats
    }
    
    class DetectionService {
        -webClient: WebClient
        -detectionRepository: DetectionRepository
        +predict(TrafficRequest) Map
        +getDashboardStats() DashboardStats
    }
    
    class PredictionEngine {
        -predictor: NIDSPredictor
        +predict_from_features(dict) dict
        +predict_from_dataframe(df) dict
    }
    
    class Detection {
        +Long id
        +String attackType
        +Double confidence
        +Severity severity
        +String explanation
    }
    
    DetectionController --> DetectionService
    DetectionService --> PredictionEngine
    DetectionService --> Detection
    PredictionEngine --> Detection
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Network Monitoring**
- Live traffic analysis with immediate threat detection
- Continuous monitoring of network flows
- Automatic alert generation for suspicious activities
- Historical trend analysis and reporting

**Advanced Machine Learning Capabilities**
- XGBoost-based intrusion detection model
- SHAP-based explainable AI for decision transparency
- Multi-class attack classification (DoS, DDoS, Brute Force, etc.)
- Confidence scoring and severity assessment

**Comprehensive Dashboard Interface**
- Interactive charts and graphs for threat visualization
- Real-time statistics and analytics
- Detailed attack analysis and reporting
- User-friendly navigation and filtering

### Implementation Highlights

**Multi-Modal Data Processing**
- `Mini_Project/ml-service/prediction_engine.py` - Advanced preprocessing pipeline
- Support for both single-flow and batch analysis
- Automatic feature engineering and normalization
- Robust error handling and validation

**Security Architecture**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java` - Token-based authentication
- Comprehensive CORS configuration for frontend integration
- Secure password hashing with bcrypt
- Role-based access control implementation

**Data Persistence Strategy**
- `Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/repository/DetectionRepository.java` - Repository pattern implementation
- Support for both H2 (development) and PostgreSQL (production)
- Automatic schema migration with Hibernate
- Efficient query optimization for analytics

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> DataUpload
    DataUpload --> Analysis: Upload Complete
    Analysis --> Prediction: Process Features
    Prediction --> Classification: ML Model
    Classification --> AlertGeneration: High Confidence
    Classification --> Storage: Low Confidence
    AlertGeneration --> Notification: Critical Threat
    Storage --> [*]: Record Saved
    Notification --> [*]: Alert Sent
```

**Dataset Analysis Pipeline**
- Parquet file upload and validation
- Automated feature extraction and preprocessing
- Batch prediction execution
- SHAP explanation generation
- Comprehensive statistical analysis

**Live Traffic Monitoring**
- Real-time flow capture and processing
- Immediate prediction and alerting
- Continuous monitoring without manual intervention
- Scalable architecture for high-throughput environments

**Reporting and Visualization**
- Automated PDF report generation
- Interactive dashboard with drill-down capabilities
- Export functionality for compliance and documentation
- Customizable analytics views

---

**Sources:**
- [ClinicalNidsApplication.java](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/ClinicalNidsApplication.java)
- [SecurityConfig.java](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/config/SecurityConfig.java)
- [DetectionController.java](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/controller/DetectionController.java)
- [DetectionService.java](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/service/DetectionService.java)
- [prediction_engine.py](Mini_Project/ml-service/prediction_engine.py)
- [app.py](Mini_Project/ml-service/app.py)
- [application.properties](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/resources/application.properties)
- [pom.xml](Mini_Project/backend/pom.xml)
- [package.json](Mini_Project/clinical-nids-dashboard/package.json)
- [requirements.txt](Mini_Project/ml-service/requirements.txt)
- [App.jsx](Mini_Project/clinical-nids-dashboard/src/App.jsx)
- [Layout.jsx](Mini_Project/clinical-nids-dashboard/src/components/Layout.jsx)
- [start_all.bat](Mini_Project/start_all.bat)
- [Detection.java](Mini_Project/backend/src/main/java/com/clinicalnids/backend/entity/Detection.java)

</blog>
